methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Trilobio is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Roya Amini-Naieni and Maximilian Schommer, with $11M in total capital raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 public comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers within Biotech & Health, though the narrow peer set limits the applicability of certain methods.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, leveraging the $11M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $28M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage peer identified). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because target revenue is not disclosed; the 3 public Biotech & Health comps trade at a 7.8x P/S median, but this multiple is irrelevant without the target's revenue figure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, centered at $28M. This range is derived entirely from stage-progression methodology and should be weighted accordingly. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier to Trilobio's specific risk profile, the absence of direct comparable company data at seed stage, and lack of revenue visibility. Additional disclosure of burn rate, cash runway, or clinical/technical milestones would strengthen conviction in this estimate.
